title: Can't see containers in WSL podman machine via podman command run in the machine
body:
### Issue Description

After upgrading to podman 5.0.2 from 4.9 I am unable to run:
`wsl -d podman-machine-default enterns podman container list` to see the list of containers.
This also means I can't `wsl -d podman-machine-default enterns podman container cp` to work around podman container cp not working on the windows host directly. (see https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14862)



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Upgrade to podman 5.0.2 via podman desktop. Let podman desktop to clean up the old machine and setup the new one.
2. Create a container via podman commands run on the windows host.
3. Run `wsl -d podman-machine-default enterns podman container list` to get the list of accessible machines that a 'podman container cp' could be run against.


### Describe the results you received

No containers are visible in the Container list.

### Describe the results you expected

All containers created on the windows host would be visible inside the podman machine.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I am running on a windows 10 machine via wsl and podman-desktop.
WSL version: 2.1.5.0
Kernel version: 5.15.146.1-2
WSLg version: 1.0.60
MSRDC version: 1.2.5105
Direct3D version: 1.611.1-81528511
DXCore version: 10.0.25131.1002-220531-1700.rs-onecore-base2-hyp
Windows version: 10.0.19045.4291

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Does it work with `podman machine ssh`?
There are two ways to create the machine rootless/rootful that decides under which user the containers are run in the VM. So if you setup rootless then when you enter you need to make sure to switch to the users in the VM otherwise run as root.

> Does it work with `podman machine ssh`? There are two ways to create the machine rootless/rootful that decides under which user the containers are run in the VM. So if you setup rootless then when you enter you need to make sure to switch to the users in the VM otherwise run as root.

That was my issue! I created the machine as rootful this time around. Thanks for the help!
